spec_match.rationale: Demo probes for `navigator.mediaDevices.getAllScreensMedia`, calls it on click, renders one card per returned MediaStreamTrack, and surfaces the single-call multi-screen contract without hiding the IWA/policy gate.

interactive.rationale: Real getAllScreensMedia and getDisplayMedia buttons, a labeled demo-stream path, per-track simulate-unplug controls, live status, video previews, settings readouts, and log output all update in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index now promises per-track settings and track.onended lifecycle changes, which the page delivers alongside the original probe and attempted call.

edge_cases.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP reproduced the missing settings/lifecycle UI, then verified the repair: unsupported API rejection stays clean, two demo streams render settings rows, simulate-unplug fires the track.onended path, readyState updates to ended, and a stubbed getAllScreensMedia/getDisplayMedia success branch renders the same track cards.

references.rationale: See also links to chromestatus.com/feature/6284029979525120 and the Capture-All-Screens spec.

design_system.rationale: Controls and track cards use design-system variables, every button background override sets color, and the getDisplayMedia fallback now has secondary styling distinct from the primary capture action.

summary: The base getAllScreensMedia demo now keeps the real API attempt and fallback path, adds per-track getSettings() summaries, handles track.onended with visible ended-state cards, includes deterministic demo streams for unsupported normal origins, and visually distinguishes the fallback action. Tested with Chrome DevTools MCP on the real unsupported page, a stubbed success page for both capture buttons, and the feature conformance route.
